sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Desolation Day Use Permits & Parking — Eldorado National Forest (USDA Forest Service)
sources.quotedText: Day use is not subject to fees nor limited by quota at any time of the year.

sources.title: Hemlock Lake | El Dorado County | Desolation Wilderness — Sierrafish.com
sources.quotedText: Hemlock Lake is a self-sustaining Brook Trout fishery in Desolation Wilderness.

parameters.note: Hemlock Lake, an alpine lake in the Desolation Wilderness (El Dorado Co.) reached from Wrights Lake via the Grouse Lake Trail on the west side of the Crystal Range, is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1). It is not a Lake Tahoe tributary (it drains west toward Wrights Lake / the South Fork American River, not into Lake Tahoe); and in any case the §7.50(b)(152) Lake Tahoe tributary rule covers only tributary streams up to the first lake and the 300-ft tributary-mouth zone, not standing lakes.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: Hemlock Lake lies inside Desolation Wilderness (Wrights Lake / Grouse Lake Trail access). A USDA Forest Service wilderness permit is required year-round for overnight use; a quota applies to overnight destination zones from the Friday before Memorial Day through September 30. Day use is free and unlimited by quota, with a self-issue permit required only when permit boxes are installed at the trailheads (day-use permits are not required in winter, roughly October–March). Bear canisters are required for all overnight visitors.

raw.overnight_quota_season: Friday before Memorial Day through September 30
raw.day_use: free, no quota; self-issue permit when trailhead boxes installed

regulations.citation: USDA Forest Service — Desolation Wilderness permit program
regulations.humanSummary: Desolation Wilderness permit: required year-round for overnight use (quota Fri. before Memorial Day–Sep. 30); day use free and un-quota'd, self-issue permit when trailhead boxes are installed.
regulations.verbatimText: Permits are required year-round for overnight use.

parameters.description: Fishery note. A Sierra Nevada lake-fishing database (Sierrafish.com) describes Hemlock Lake as 'a self-sustaining Brook Trout fishery in Desolation Wilderness,' reporting brook trout caught in the 9–11 inch range. CDFW does not list Hemlock Lake in its 2026 Provisional Stocking plan and no longer stocks it; any brook trout are a self-sustaining population descended from historical plantings, not a current put-and-take fishery. This is a third-party angler report, not a CDFW fish survey.

regulations.citation: Sierrafish.com — Hemlock Lake (Desolation Wilderness); CDFW 2026 Provisional Stocking Document (Hemlock Lake not listed)
regulations.humanSummary: Reported as a self-sustaining brook-trout water by a third-party angler database; not currently stocked by CDFW.
regulations.verbatimText: Hemlock Lake is a self-sustaining Brook Trout fishery in Desolation Wilderness.
